top_categories.workout_context: 621 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 621 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
35,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
621 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Across Spain's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
At Race Across Spain, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 35,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
56ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
35,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Across Spain. Train it until it feels boring.
Race Across Spain's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 621 miles.
Tempo is Race Across Spain's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 621 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 621 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 621 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Race Across Spain with 35,000ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and VO2max to prepare you for 621 miles of mixed terrain in Santander to Valencia, Spain.
